kill_fact: No clean kill_fact breaks the thesis: every load-bearing FACT in the debate verifies against primary sources (ITC Final Determination Feb-13-2025 on patents 9,516,761/9,166,481 + Limited Exclusion Order; Q1'26 revenue $113.0M +20.2% YoY, backlog $300.6M +70% QoQ, B:B>2.0, GM 55.2%, cash $404.2M, debt-free, FY26 guide ~$570M; SynQor $28.56M paid 2026-03-18; FY25 $45M settlement; ~107x trailing P/E / ~$14.8B cap / +494% TTM off a $41.76 52-wk low). The thesis is a CAUTIOUS 'C' (price front-ran an already-FIRED catalyst at a valuation above even base-case SoP) and that bear case SURVIVES verification: the ITC re-rate is spent, the stock has quintupled, valuation (~107x P/E, ~26x FY26E sales) sits above the ~$215 base-case SoP, the royalty annuity is explicitly 'through 2026' with new deals gated on a distant 2027 ITC resolution, and Vicor itself PAID SynQor $28.6M (verified, 10-Q) — litigation cuts both ways. BUT the bear's single most-cited earnings-quality kill is FABRICATED and must be corrected (see corrections), so the thesis is confirmed only WITH that correction, not on the bear's own numbers.
corrections: LOAD-BEARING ERROR in bear_thesis / interrogator_dossier / moderator_conclusion / valley_of_death / consensus_delta: the claim that 'Q1'26 $0.44 GAAP EPS was flattered by a $27.3M tax benefit (pre-tax operating income only ~$16.9M)' is FALSE per the Q1'26 10-Q / press release. Actual: pre-tax income $20.403M, income-tax BENEFIT of only $273K (NOT $27.3M — a ~100x digit-transposition), net income $20.664M, diluted EPS $0.44. Operating income was $16.884M; the '~$16.9M' figure is OPERATING income, not pre-tax. Q1'26 earnings were essentially all operationally earned and were NOT meaningfully tax-flattered. Secondary correction: royalty revenue WAS disclosed separately ($14.965M in Q1'26), contradicting the 'royalty vs product split bundled by management' claim. Net effect: earnings quality is BETTER than the bear alleged, which modestly RAISES the productive floor — but it does not move the valuation/already-fired-catalyst kills, so the cautious verdict and low conviction stand. FY25's $45M one-time settlement and the +23% royalty step-up flattering FY25 +26% growth remain valid bear points (verified).
evidence: Vicor IR / GlobeNewswire, 2025-02-14: 'ITC bars importation of power modules and unlicensed computing systems that infringe Vicor patents' — Inv. No. 337-TA-1370, U.S. Patents 9,516,761 & 9,166,481 found valid/infringed, Limited Exclusion Order + Cease-and-Desist issued (load-bearing catalyst, already FIRED).

Vicor Q1 2026 10-Q (qtr ended 2026-03-31, via StockTitan SEC-filings summary): net revenue $113.0M, pre-tax income $20.403M, income-tax BENEFIT $273K, net income $20.664M, diluted EPS $0.44, operating income $16.884M, GM 55.2% ($62.366M), royalty revenue $14.965M, cash $404.245M, no long-term debt — REFUTES the '$27.3M tax benefit' bear claim.

Vicor Q1 2026 10-Q / earnings call (2026-05): SynQor judgment of $28,557,256 paid 2026-03-18 (Federal Circuit affirmed 2026-02-13); Q1'26 operating cash flow -$3.9M net of that payment — confirms litigation cuts both ways and the royalty annuity is two-sided.

Market data (Yahoo/CNN, June 2026): VICR $328.04, market cap ~$14.8B, trailing P/E ~107x, 52-wk range $41.76-$361.89 (~+494% TTM) — confirms valuation sits above the ~$215 base-case SoP and the already-ran/already-fired risk.
